markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Close the canvas gap (Obsidian canvas-class on Loom): infinite-canvas board where existing LoomBlocks/notes/files are PLACED (references, never copies - drag from Loom views/quick switcher), free text cards (which ARE new note blocks), edges between placed items (which ARE Loom edges where semantic, visual-only where not - distinguished explicitly), pan/zoom/select/group, canvas itself stored as a typed block (board state in Postgres, projection rules apply).
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.
Bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries only (Tiptap/Monaco precedent); no CDN; no external app launches as product behavior.
PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; UI artifacts are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle (v02.193), product code, tests, and receipts.

OPERATOR ORDER (DEC-007, 2026-06-12): NO scaffolding, templates, mock-ups, or vibecoding; every feature must be working AND tested in runtime (executed vitest/Playwright/real-PG proof per feature, not status text). A feature without an executed runtime proof is NOT done.
Placing a block on a canvas creates a reference, NEVER a content copy (negative test).
Canvas state is a typed LoomBlock; deleting a canvas never deletes the blocks placed on it (negative test).
scope.expected_behavior: Operator arranges real blocks spatially, draws connections, and the canvas persists - an Obsidian-canvas-class surface over LoomBlock authority.
acceptance_criteria: Text card becomes a real note block (proven).
Adversarial self-review recorded per the WP loop; findings fixed before evidence write.
verification: cargo test -p handshake_core --features runtime-full,test-utils (scoped) green with real-PG tests for backend state.
pnpm vitest run green; tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean.
Offline Playwright runtime proof for each named capability (zero external network requests).
handoff_requirements: List files changed, tests run with exact names/counts, commits.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence and Playwright artifacts produced.
List remaining risks and any honestly-documented deviations (e.g., prose multi-cursor approach).

evidence: commit ff9295fe on feat/WP-KERNEL-009 (16 files, migration 0334_loom_canvas_board), zero .GOV

runtime proof: 7 real-PG tests PASS (227s); vitest 6 (live-preview reference-not-copy, remove-placement keeps source block, free-text-card IS a real note block, semantic edge appears in loom graph vs visual-only edge does NOT); tsc/eslint clean; offline Playwright 1 PASS (2.1m, real backend fixture mt261_loom_canvas_fixture on isolated PG schema)

canvas IS a typed LoomBlock(content_type=canvas); placements are FK block-id references (never content copies — proven by remove-placement + edit-source-reflects); semantic edges are real loom_edges (graph authority); visual-only edges are board-local decoration explicitly NOT graph authority; board viewport JSONB carries an EventLedger receipt; not conflated with the legacy Excalidraw sketch canvas

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-261 requires an Obsidian-canvas-class board surface over LoomBlock authority: place existing blocks as references (never copies), free-text cards as real note blocks, semantic edges as real loom_edges vs visual-only board decoration, pan/zoom/group/persist, with the canvas itself a typed LoomBlock and board state in PostgreSQL+EventLedger. The implementation delivers exactly this with real schema, real sqlx storage, and real runtime proof at all three layers (real-PG cargo tests, vitest, offline real-backend Playwright).
findings: Migration 0334_loom_canvas_boards.sql: canvas IS a loom_blocks(content_type='canvas') row (block_id PK -> loom_blocks FK); placements FK placed_block_id ON DELETE RESTRICT + UNIQUE(canvas_block_id, placed_block_id) enforce reference-not-copy at the DB level; board CASCADE deletes only board/placements/visual_edges, never referenced loom_blocks. Pure PostgreSQL, no SQLite/external engine (DEC-008 honored).

storage/postgres.rs:7914 place_block_on_canvas is real sqlx INSERT with same-workspace validation, UUID v7 placement_id (LCP-...), FK reference to placed block; create/get/update/remove/visual-edge/viewport methods at :7729-8090 are all real SQL. No placeholders/todo!/unimplemented!/Unavailable on the canvas path (the only 'stub' grep hits at :485-510 are pre-existing unrelated WorkPacketStatus/TaskBoardStatus enum string mappings).

tests/loom_canvas_board_tests.rs: 7 real-PG tests against the migrated knowledge_pg schema cover every acceptance criterion: canvas-is-loom-block+EventLedger-receipt (:120), place/move/group/visual-edge/viewport round-trip (:154), remove-placement-keeps-source negative (:261), delete-canvas-keeps-placed-blocks negative (:300), edit-source-reflects-through-placement live-reference (:350), semantic-edge-in-graph vs visual-only-not-in-graph (:399), free-text-card-is-real-note-block (:501).

app/src/components/LoomCanvasBoard.test.tsx: 6 vitest cases assert FE wiring matches the reference semantics: live previews via getLoomBlock (reference not copy) (:109), removeCanvasPlacement without touching source (:119), createCanvasCard -> content_type=note (:126), SEMANTIC connection -> createLoomEdge and NOT addCanvasVisualEdge (:136), VISUAL-ONLY -> addCanvasVisualEdge and NOT createLoomEdge (:153), pan/zoom persisted via viewport endpoint (:169).

tests/visual/mt261-loom-canvas-real-backend.spec.ts: spawns the real mt261_loom_canvas_fixture backend binary on an isolated PG schema, routes/aborts and records any request, and asserts externalRequests == [] (:281) — genuine offline real-backend proof, zero external network.

Self-certification present (lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER also signed completion), but per Spec-Realism sub-rule-3 self-cert alone is not a fail when the proof is real and independently meaningful, which it is here.

Aside (non-blocking): packet cites v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194; the canvas surface concept (Docs & Canvas / canvas-based planning boards in product-surfaces module) resolves in current spec. Paperwork-only, not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger and/or offline Playwright where the contract names durable state or GUI behavior.
